italy.name: Classic Individual Tiramisu
italy.description: A rich, velvety Italian dessert layered with espresso-soaked ladyfingers and airy mascarpone cream, perfectly portioned for one to provide an instant pick-me-up.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: Combine the cooled espresso and coffee liqueur in a shallow dish. Set aside.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: In a small mixing bowl, vigorously whisk the pasteurized egg yolk and granulated sugar together until the mixture becomes pale yellow and slightly thickened. Note: Use commercially pasteurized egg yolks for safety, as this raw egg mixture will not be cooked to the standard safe temperature of 74C/165F.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: Add the cold mascarpone cheese to the egg yolk mixture. Gently fold them together using a spatula just until smooth.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: In a separate clean bowl, whip the heavy cream until medium peaks form. Gently fold the whipped cream into the mascarpone mixture to create a light, airy mousse.

steps.title: assemble
steps.instruction: Quickly dip the broken ladyfinger pieces into the espresso mixture for exactly one second per side.

steps.title: assemble
steps.instruction: Place a layer of the espresso-dipped ladyfingers at the bottom of your serving glass. Spoon half of the mascarpone cream evenly over the ladyfingers.

steps.title: assemble
steps.instruction: Add another layer of dipped ladyfingers, then top with the remaining mascarpone cream. Smooth the top surface with the back of a spoon.

steps.title: finish
steps.instruction: Cover the glass and chill in the refrigerator at 4C/39F for at least 4 hours to allow the flavors to meld and the cream to firm up.

steps.title: finish
steps.instruction: Right before serving, use a fine mesh strainer to dust the top of the tiramisu generously with unsweetened cocoa powder.

italy.name: Limoncello Cocktail
italy.description: A bright citrus cocktail balancing tart lemon and sweet limoncello with nutty hazelnut notes and a frothy egg white texture.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: Pour the limoncello, hazelnut liqueur, simple syrup, lemon juice, and egg white into the cocktail shaker.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Seal the shaker and shake the contents vigorously without ice for 20 seconds to emulsify the egg white.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Add a handful of ice to the shaker and shake again for 15 seconds until the outside of the vessel is chilled.

steps.title: finish
steps.instruction: Strain the liquid through a Hawthorne strainer into a chilled Nick and Nora coupe glass.

steps.title: finish
steps.instruction: Place the lace biscuit on the rim of the glass or carefully on top of the foam to garnish.

italy.name: Classic Caprese Salad
italy.description: A vibrant, refreshing Italian salad featuring ripe tomatoes, creamy fresh mozzarella, and sweet basil leaves, finished with a drizzle of quality olive oil and a pinch of flaky sea salt.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: Using a sharp chef's knife or serrated knife, slice the tomatoes and fresh mozzarella into even rounds, approximately one centimeter thick.

steps.title: assemble
steps.instruction: Arrange the tomato and mozzarella slices on a serving platter, alternating them in a slightly overlapping circular or linear pattern.

steps.title: assemble
steps.instruction: Tuck the fresh basil leaves evenly between the overlapping slices of tomato and mozzarella.

steps.title: finish
steps.instruction: Sprinkle the arranged salad evenly with the flaky sea salt and freshly ground black pepper.

steps.title: finish
steps.instruction: Drizzle the extra virgin olive oil generously over the entire platter just before serving.

italy.name: Hearty Minestrone Soup
italy.description: A comforting, thick, and vibrant Italian vegetable soup simmering with seasonal vegetables, creamy cannellini beans, and tender small pasta in a rich tomato-herb broth.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: Prepare all vegetables by dicing the onion, carrots, celery, and zucchini into uniform, bite-sized pieces to ensure even cooking.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Heat the extra virgin olive oil in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium heat. Add the diced onion, carrots, and celery. Sauté until the vegetables are softened and the onions are translucent, about 7 to 8 minutes.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Stir in the minced garlic, dried oregano, and dried basil. Cook for 1 minute until highly fragrant, stirring continuously to prevent the garlic from burning.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Pour in the crushed tomatoes and vegetable broth. Increase the heat to medium-high to bring the liquid to a gentle boil around 100°C/212°F, then immediately reduce the heat to low to maintain a steady simmer.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Add the diced zucchini, green beans, kosher salt, and black pepper. Cover the pot partially and simmer for 15 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the vegetables to become tender.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Stir in the drained cannellini beans and the dry small pasta. Continue to simmer uncovered for another 10 to 12 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the pasta is cooked al dente.

steps.title: finish
steps.instruction: Remove the pot from the heat. Taste the broth and adjust the seasoning with more salt or pepper if necessary. Ladle into warm bowls and top with a sprinkle of grated vegetarian hard cheese if desired.

italy.name: Dulce de Leche Panna Cotta
italy.description: A velvety, eggless Italian custard infused with rich, caramelized dulce de leche. This five-ingredient dessert melts in the mouth, delivering a perfect balance of milky sweetness and roasted caramel notes.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: Pour the cold milk into a small bowl and sprinkle the gelatin powder evenly over the surface. Let it sit undisturbed for 5 to 10 minutes to bloom and hydrate.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: In a medium saucepan, combine the heavy cream, 160 grams of the dulce de leche, and vanilla extract. Heat over medium heat, whisking gently, until the mixture reaches a gentle simmer at around 80 degrees Celsius (175 degrees Fahrenheit). Do not let it boil.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Remove the saucepan from the heat. Scrape the bloomed gelatin and milk mixture into the hot cream. Whisk thoroughly until the gelatin is completely dissolved and the mixture is entirely smooth.

steps.title: assemble
steps.instruction: Pour the mixture through a fine-mesh strainer into a liquid measuring cup to catch any undissolved pieces. Divide the strained liquid evenly among 4 ramekins or serving glasses.

steps.title: finish
steps.instruction: Let the ramekins cool to room temperature, then cover each with plastic wrap. Transfer to the refrigerator and chill for at least 4 hours, or until completely set.

steps.title: finish
steps.instruction: To serve, dip the bottoms of the ramekins in warm water for 5 seconds. Run a thin knife around the edges and invert onto plates. Warm the remaining dulce de leche and drizzle over the top.

italy.name: Italian Roasted Peppers with Anchovy and Caper Dressing
italy.description: A vibrant, savory antipasto featuring sweet, blistered bell peppers marinated in a rich, umami-packed garlic and anchovy infused extra virgin olive oil with briny capers.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: Preheat the oven to 230°C (450°F). Lightly rub the whole bell peppers with a few drops of olive oil and place them on the baking sheet.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Roast the peppers in the oven for 25 to 30 minutes. Use tongs to turn them every 10 minutes to ensure even exposure to the heat.

steps.title: finish
steps.instruction: Using tongs, immediately transfer the hot peppers to a mixing bowl. Cover tightly with plastic wrap or a flat plate and let them sit for 15 minutes. This creates steam that loosens the skins.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: Once cool enough to handle, peel the charred skins off the peppers and discard them. Remove the stems and seeds, then slice the flesh into long strips. Place the strips into a clean serving bowl.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: In a small skillet over medium-low heat, gently warm the 45ml of extra virgin olive oil, sliced garlic, and chopped anchovies. Cook, stirring frequently, for 3 to 4 minutes.

steps.title: assemble
steps.instruction: Pour the warm garlic and anchovy oil over the sliced peppers. Add the rinsed capers and gently toss everything together until well coated.

steps.title: finish
steps.instruction: Let the mixture marinate at room temperature for at least 25 minutes before serving to allow the flavors to meld. Serve at room temperature.

italy.name: Classic Italian Tiramisu Cups
italy.description: Rich, velvety mascarpone cream layered with espresso-soaked ladyfingers in elegant individual portions. A delicate balance of bold coffee notes and subtle sweetness with a dusting of bitter cocoa.

ingredients.name: strong espresso or coffee
ingredients.amount: 150 ml (brewed and cooled to room temperature)

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: In a shallow dish, combine the cooled espresso and Marsala wine. Set aside.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: In a mixing bowl, vigorously whisk the egg yolks and sugar together until the mixture triples in volume, becomes thick, and turns a pale yellow color.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: Add the cold mascarpone to the egg yolk mixture. Gently fold it in using a spatula until completely smooth and homogeneous. Do not overmix.

steps.title: assemble
steps.instruction: Quickly dip the ladyfingers into the coffee mixture for exactly one second per side. Break them as needed to fit the bottom of your individual serving glasses.

steps.title: assemble
steps.instruction: Spoon or pipe a layer of the mascarpone cream over the soaked ladyfingers in the glasses. Repeat with another layer of dipped ladyfingers and finish with a final layer of cream.

steps.title: finish
steps.instruction: Cover the cups and chill in the refrigerator at 4C/39F for at least 4 hours to allow the flavors to meld and the cream to set.

steps.title: finish
steps.instruction: Just before serving, use a fine mesh sieve to generously dust the tops with unsweetened cocoa powder.

italy.name: White Bean Caprese Salad
italy.description: A fresh salad combining creamy white beans with cherry tomatoes, mozzarella, and basil in a light balsamic dressing.

ingredients.name: mozzarella
ingredients.amount: 140 g (halved or torn into bite-size pieces)

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: Place the rinsed white beans, halved cherry tomatoes, mozzarella pieces, and half of the torn basil into a medium bowl.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: Pour the olive oil over the ingredients and season with kosher salt and black pepper.

steps.title: assemble
steps.instruction: Stir the mixture gently with a spoon to distribute the oil and seasonings without breaking the beans.

steps.title: finish
steps.instruction: Drizzle the balsamic vinegar over the salad and top with the remaining basil and a pinch of flaky salt.

japan.name: Spicy Tofu And Edamame Stir-Fry
japan.description: Crispy golden cubes of tofu and vibrant, tender edamame beans are tossed in a sticky, savory sauce with a bold chili heat. This vibrant stir-fry delivers a satisfying crunch and mouth-watering aroma in under thirty minutes.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: Wrap the tofu in paper towels and press using a tofu press or heavy objects for 15 minutes to extract excess moisture. Cut into bite-sized cubes.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: Place the cubed tofu in a mixing bowl and sprinkle with cornstarch. Toss gently until every piece is evenly coated.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: In a small bowl, whisk together the soy sauce, vegetarian mushroom oyster sauce, water, sugar, and toasted sesame oil to create the sauce.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Heat 20ml of the vegetable oil in a wok or large skillet over medium-high heat. Add the coated tofu cubes in a single layer. Pan-fry for 6 to 8 minutes, turning occasionally, until crispy and golden brown on all sides. Remove the tofu from the pan and set aside.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Add the remaining 10ml of vegetable oil to the wok. Add the minced garlic, grated ginger, and sliced chilies. Sauté for 30 seconds until highly fragrant, being careful not to burn the garlic.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Add the shelled edamame to the wok and stir-fry for 2 minutes to warm through and coat in the aromatics.

steps.title: assemble
steps.instruction: Return the crispy tofu to the wok. Pour the prepared sauce mixture over the ingredients. Toss vigorously for 1 minute as the sauce bubbles and thickens, clinging to the tofu and edamame.

steps.title: finish
steps.instruction: Remove from heat. Transfer to a serving platter and garnish with thinly sliced scallions and toasted sesame seeds.

japan.name: Vegetarian Japanese Miso Soup
japan.description: A comforting, savory broth brimming with umami, featuring delicate cubes of silken tofu, tender wakame seaweed, and the bright, crisp bite of fresh spring onions. This traditional daily staple warms the body and soul.

ingredients.name: dried kombu
ingredients.amount: 5 g (wiped clean with a damp cloth)

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: Place the cold water and dried kombu in a medium saucepan. Let it sit at room temperature for 15 minutes to gently extract the natural umami flavors.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: While the broth steeps, place the dried wakame seaweed in a small bowl of water to rehydrate for 5 minutes, then drain completely.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Place the saucepan with the kombu over medium heat. Just before the water reaches a boil, at approximately 90°C/195°F, remove and discard the kombu to prevent the broth from becoming bitter and slimy.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Add the cubed silken tofu and the drained, rehydrated wakame to the hot dashi. Simmer gently for 2 minutes to warm the ingredients through.

steps.title: finish
steps.instruction: Turn off the heat completely. Lower a fine mesh strainer slightly into the hot broth, add the miso paste into the strainer, and use a spoon or chopsticks to dissolve it seamlessly into the soup.

steps.title: finish
steps.instruction: Ladle the hot soup carefully into serving bowls, taking care not to break the delicate tofu cubes. Garnish with thinly sliced spring onions and serve immediately.

japan.name: Sesame-Chile Ramen Noodle Salad With Bok Choy
japan.description: Chewy ramen noodles, crisp bok choy, and sweet corn mingle in a rich, nutty sesame dressing cut with the fiery tang of chile crisp and black vinegar. This vibrant, cooling salad is highly adaptable and delivers deep savory flavors with every bite.

ingredients.name: baby bok choy
ingredients.amount: 250 g (base trimmed, leaves separated and halved lengthways)

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: In a medium mixing bowl, vigorously whisk together the chinese sesame paste, soy sauce, chinkiang black vinegar, toasted sesame oil, granulated sugar, and minced garlic until a thick paste forms. Gradually whisk in the warm water until the dressing is smooth and evenly emulsified. Stir in the chile crisp and set aside to allow the flavors to meld.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Fill a large pot with water and bring to a rapid rolling boil at 100°C/212°F. Submerge the dry ramen noodles and cook according to package directions, typically about 3 minutes, stirring occasionally with chopsticks or a fork to separate the strands.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: During the final 1 minute of the noodles' cooking time, drop the prepared baby bok choy and sweet corn kernels directly into the boiling water to blanch. The water temperature will drop slightly, but continue cooking until the bok choy stalks turn vibrant green.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: Drain the contents of the pot immediately into a colander. Rinse aggressively under cold running water for at least 1 minute, tossing the noodles and vegetables by hand until they are completely cool to the touch. This halts the cooking process and removes excess surface starch.

steps.title: assemble
steps.instruction: Transfer the drained noodles, bok choy, and corn to a large serving bowl. Pour the prepared sesame-chile dressing over the mixture, add the sliced scallions and chopped cilantro, and toss thoroughly until every noodle is evenly coated in the sauce. Serve immediately or chill.

japan.name: Vibrant Trio of Meal Prep Grain Bowls
japan.description: A versatile week of meal prep featuring a fluffy quinoa base partitioned into three distinct flavor profiles: a zesty Mediterranean chickpea bowl, a smoky Southwestern black bean bowl, and a savory sesame edamame bowl. Bright, nourishing, and designed to prevent flavor fatigue.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: Place the quinoa in a fine mesh sieve and rinse thoroughly under cold running water for at least 30 seconds to remove the bitter coating.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: In a medium saucepan, combine the rinsed quinoa, vegetable broth, 15ml olive oil, and 3g salt. Bring to a boil over medium-high heat at 100 degrees Celsius or 212 degrees Fahrenheit, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer until the liquid is absorbed and the quinoa is tender.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: Remove the quinoa from heat and let it rest covered for 5 minutes. Fluff with a fork and spread it on a baking sheet to cool completely.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: While the quinoa cools, prepare the Mediterranean dressing by vigorously whisking together tahini, lemon juice, and cold water in a small bowl until smooth. Add a pinch of salt to taste.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: Prepare the Southwestern dressing by whisking together 30ml olive oil, lime juice, and ground cumin in a second small bowl.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: Prepare the Asian-inspired dressing by whisking together the tamari, toasted sesame oil, rice vinegar, and maple syrup in a third small bowl.

steps.title: assemble
steps.instruction: Divide the cooled quinoa evenly among 6 meal prep containers, placing approximately 150g of cooked quinoa into each.

steps.title: assemble
steps.instruction: Build two Mediterranean bowls by topping the quinoa with chickpeas, cucumber, cherry tomatoes, and kalamata olives. Pack the tahini dressing in separate small containers.

steps.title: assemble
steps.instruction: Build two Southwestern bowls by topping the quinoa with black beans, corn, and diced red bell pepper. Pack the lime-cumin dressing in separate small containers.

steps.title: assemble
steps.instruction: Build two Asian-inspired bowls by topping the quinoa with edamame, shredded carrots, and red cabbage. Pack the sesame-tamari dressing in separate small containers.

japan.name: Spring Chicken Miso Soup
japan.description: A clear chicken soup flavored with miso and tamari, served with buckwheat noodles, sugar snap peas, and leeks.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: Season the chicken thighs evenly with salt and black pepper.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Heat vegetable oil in a soup pot over medium-high heat (190°C/375°F). Sear the chicken thighs for 4 minutes per side, adjusting heat to prevent excessive browning.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Add minced garlic and grated ginger to the pot. Sauté for 1 minute until fragrant.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Stir in the mirin, sugar, tamari, and water.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Bring the liquid to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer gently for 20 minutes until the chicken reaches an internal temperature of 74°C/165°F.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: In a separate saucepan, boil the soba noodles until al dente according to package directions. Drain and rinse with cool water.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: In a small pot of boiling water, blanch the diced leeks and trimmed sugar snap peas for 1 minute. Drain and rinse with cool water.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: Remove the chicken from the soup pot and cut into 1.5 cm (1/2 inch) pieces.

steps.title: finish
steps.instruction: Whisk the miso in a small bowl with a small amount of warm broth (60°C/140°F) until the mixture is smooth.

steps.title: finish
steps.instruction: Return the chopped chicken to the soup pot. Stir in the miso mixture, baby spinach, blanched leeks, and snap peas. Heat for 1 minute until the spinach is wilted.

steps.title: finish
steps.instruction: Place the cooked soba noodles into bowls. Ladle the hot soup over the noodles and garnish with julienned basil.

japan.name: Classic Korean Army Stew (Budae Jjigae)
japan.description: A deeply comforting, spicy, and savory fusion stew combining traditional Korean ingredients like aged kimchi and gochujang with processed meats like Spam and hot dogs, finished with chewy instant ramen noodles.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: In a small mixing bowl, combine the gochujang, gochugaru, soy sauce, mirin, and minced garlic. Stir vigorously until a cohesive paste forms, then set it aside.

steps.title: assemble
steps.instruction: In a shallow wide pot or braiser, arrange the chopped kimchi, sliced spam, hot dogs, ground pork, firm tofu, yellow onion, scallions, and baked beans in separate neat clusters around the edge. Place the mixed seasoning paste in the center of the pot. Ensure you handle the raw ground pork with separate utensils and wash your hands to prevent cross-contamination.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Gently pour the anchovy-kelp stock over the arranged ingredients. Bring the stew to a boil over medium-high heat, which is approximately 100°C/212°F. As it begins to boil, gently stir the seasoning paste in the center into the broth until fully dissolved, taking care not to mix up all the arranged ingredients.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Once the stew is boiling rapidly, add the instant ramen noodles and soaked rice cakes to the center of the pot. Place the slice of American cheese directly on top of the noodles. Let everything simmer undisturbed for 4 to 5 minutes until the noodles are chewy and the raw ground pork is fully cooked, reaching a safe internal temperature of 74°C/165°F.

steps.title: finish
steps.instruction: Remove the pot from the heat. Traditionally, the entire pot is placed in the center of the dining table for sharing. Serve immediately, prioritizing the ramen noodles before they soak up too much broth.

japan.name: Homemade Sushi: Nigiri and Maki Rolls
japan.description: Delicate, perfectly seasoned grains of sushi rice pair harmoniously with fresh seafood and crisp vegetables. This foundational recipe guides you through crafting elegant nigiri and classic maki rolls in your own kitchen.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Rinse the sushi rice in a fine-mesh strainer under cold water until the water runs completely clear. Drain well and place in a heavy-bottomed pot with the measured filtered water. Let soak for 30 minutes, then bring to a boil over medium-high heat. Once boiling, reduce to a low simmer, cover tightly, and cook for 15 minutes. Remove from heat and let steam, covered, for 10 minutes.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: While the rice cooks, combine the rice vinegar, sugar, and fine sea salt in a small saucepan. Heat gently over low heat, stirring constantly, until the sugar and salt completely dissolve. Do not allow the mixture to boil. Set aside to cool to room temperature.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: Transfer the hot cooked rice to a large wide bowl. Pour the cooled vinegar mixture evenly over the rice. Using a rice paddle, gently slice through the rice at an angle and fold it over, being careful not to smash the grains. Fan the rice simultaneously to cool it quickly to body temperature (around 37°C/98°F), which gives it a glossy finish.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: Prepare your fillings using strict hygiene practices for raw fish. Keep fish chilled until cutting. Use separate cutting boards for fish and vegetables to prevent cross-contamination. For nigiri, cut thin, wide rectangular slices of salmon and tuna at a 45-degree angle. For maki rolls, cut the remaining fish into long, 1cm thick strips. Have your julienned cucumber and sliced avocado ready.

steps.title: assemble
steps.instruction: To assemble maki rolls, place a half-sheet of nori rough-side up on a bamboo mat. Lightly wet your hands with tezu (a mix of water and a splash of vinegar) to prevent sticking. Spread a thin, even layer of about 70g of rice over the nori, leaving a bare 1cm border at the top edge. Arrange your matchstick fillings horizontally across the center of the rice.

steps.title: assemble
steps.instruction: Lift the bottom edge of the bamboo mat nearest you and roll it tightly over the fillings, tucking the edge over the filling. Apply gentle, even pressure to shape the roll into a firm cylinder. Roll forward again to seal the bare edge of the nori against the roll. Squeeze gently to set the shape, then unroll the mat.

steps.title: assemble
steps.instruction: To assemble nigiri, wet your hands with tezu and scoop about 15g of seasoned rice. Gently squeeze and shape it into a small, compact rectangular oval. Place a slice of fish over the rice, optionally adding a tiny dab of wasabi between the fish and rice. Press lightly with two fingers to adhere the fish to the rice mound.

steps.title: finish
steps.instruction: Using a very sharp knife wiped with a damp cloth between every single cut, slice each maki roll in half, then cut each half into three equal pieces, yielding six pieces per roll. Arrange the maki and nigiri elegantly on a platter. Serve immediately alongside soy sauce, wasabi paste, and pickled ginger.

japan.name: Vegan Eggplant And Tofu Zha Jiang Mian
japan.description: A deeply savory, umami-rich vegan take on classic Zha Jiang Mian. Caramelized eggplant, crumbled firm tofu, and earthy shiitake mushrooms are simmered in a glossy sweet bean sauce, served over thick udon noodles with crisp, cooling cucumber.

ingredients.name: firm tofu
ingredients.amount: 300 g (pressed and crumbled into small pieces)

ingredients.name: scallions
ingredients.amount: 30 g (white and green parts separated, thinly sliced)

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: Using a chef's knife, prepare all vegetables. Dice the eggplant and shiitake mushrooms into uniform 5 millimeter cubes. Mince the garlic and ginger, and slice the scallions, keeping the white and green parts separate.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: In a small mixing bowl, combine the sweet bean sauce, ground bean sauce, light soy sauce, dark soy sauce, sugar, and water. Stir thoroughly until a smooth, well-integrated liquid slurry forms.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Heat the neutral oil in a wok over medium-high heat. Add the crumbled tofu and pan-fry until the moisture evaporates and the edges turn lightly golden and firm.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Add the minced garlic, ginger, and scallion whites to the wok with the tofu. Sauté briefly until highly fragrant, then fold in the diced eggplant and shiitake mushrooms. Continue to cook until the eggplant softens and noticeably reduces in volume.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Pour the mixed sauce slurry into the wok. Stir continuously, allowing the fermented pastes to fry in the residual oil. This step is crucial for mellowing the raw, earthy flavor of the bean pastes.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Lower the heat to a gentle bubble. Simmer the mixture until the liquid reduces into a thick, deeply glossy sauce that clings heavily to the vegetables. The oil should begin to separate slightly from the sauce around the edges.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: While the sauce simmers, bring a large pot of water to a rolling boil (100°C/212°F). Cook the udon noodles according to package instructions until tender but maintaining a chewy bite. Drain thoroughly.

steps.title: finish
steps.instruction: Divide the hot, drained udon noodles among serving bowls. Spoon a generous ladle of the hot eggplant and tofu sauce directly into the center of each bowl. Garnish with a small handful of julienned cucumber and the reserved green scallion slices. Serve immediately to be tossed vigorously by the diner.

morocco.name: Classic Moroccan Harira Soup
morocco.description: A deeply warming, aromatic vegetable soup featuring tender lentils and chickpeas in a rich, spiced tomato broth, finished with a bright squeeze of lemon and fresh cilantro.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat. Add the diced onion, celery, and carrots. Sauté until the vegetables are softened and translucent, about 8 minutes.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Stir in the minced garlic, grated ginger, turmeric, cumin, cinnamon, black pepper, and kosher salt. Cook continuously until highly fragrant.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Pour in the crushed tomatoes and vegetable broth, then stir in the rinsed brown lentils. Increase the heat to high and bring the mixture to a rolling boil at approximately 100 degrees C or 212 degrees F.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and maintain a gentle simmer at approximately 90 degrees C or 195 degrees F. Cook until the lentils are completely tender but not falling apart.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Add the drained chickpeas to the pot. Stir to combine and continue to simmer uncovered to allow the flavors to meld.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: In a small mixing bowl, vigorously whisk together the cornstarch and cold water until a completely smooth slurry forms with no lumps.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Gradually pour the cornstarch slurry into the gently boiling soup while stirring constantly. Continue to cook and stir until the broth thickens to a velvety consistency.

steps.title: finish
steps.instruction: Remove the pot from the heat. Stir in the freshly chopped cilantro, parsley, and lemon juice. Taste the broth and add additional salt or lemon juice if necessary before serving.

morocco.name: Classic Moroccan Couscous with Roasted Vegetables and Harissa
morocco.description: A vibrant, fragrant, and hearty vegetarian dish featuring fluffy steamed couscous, deeply roasted root vegetables, tender chickpeas, and a spicy kick of harissa.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: Preheat the oven to 200 degrees Celsius or 400 degrees Fahrenheit.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: In a large mixing bowl, combine the carrots, sweet potato, red onion wedges, and chickpeas.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: Drizzle the mixed vegetables and chickpeas with 30ml of olive oil, then add the ras el hanout, minced garlic, and salt. Toss thoroughly to coat.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Spread the seasoned vegetables and chickpeas evenly onto a baking sheet. Roast in the preheated oven at 200 degrees Celsius or 400 degrees Fahrenheit for 20 minutes.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Remove the baking sheet from the oven briefly, add the zucchini slices, toss everything gently, and return to the oven for another 15 minutes until all vegetables are tender and slightly caramelized.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: While the vegetables finish roasting, pour the vegetable broth into a saucepan and bring to a boil over high heat.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Remove the boiling broth from the heat. Stir in the dry couscous and the remaining 15ml of olive oil, then immediately cover the saucepan with a tight-fitting lid.

steps.title: finish
steps.instruction: Let the couscous steep undisturbed off the heat for 5 minutes.

steps.title: finish
steps.instruction: Remove the lid from the saucepan and gently fluff the couscous with a fork to separate the individual grains.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: In a small bowl, whisk the harissa paste with a tablespoon of warm water to loosen it into a drizzle-able sauce.

steps.title: assemble
steps.instruction: Spread the fluffed couscous onto a large serving platter or divide among individual bowls. Top generously with the roasted vegetables and chickpeas.

steps.title: finish
steps.instruction: Drizzle the loosened harissa sauce over the top and scatter the freshly chopped parsley evenly across the dish.

mexico.name: Crispy Fried Guacamole Bites
mexico.description: Experience a striking textural contrast with these appetizers: a hot, shatteringly crisp seltzer and corn flour shell that gives way to a pleasantly cool, creamy, and vibrant jalapeno-spiked guacamole center.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: Halve the avocados, remove the pits, and scoop the flesh into a medium mixing bowl. Use a fork to crush the avocado until mostly smooth but still retaining some small chunks.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: Add the finely diced jalapeno, red onion, chopped cilantro, lime juice, and salt to the crushed avocado. Stir thoroughly to combine into a thick guacamole.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: Line a baking sheet with parchment paper. Using a small cookie scoop, shape the guacamole into 20 bite-sized mounds, placing them evenly on the prepared sheet.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: Transfer the baking sheet to the freezer. Freeze the guacamole mounds until completely solid, which will take about 2 hours.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Add the neutral oil to a heavy-bottomed pot, ensuring it is at least 5 centimeters deep. Heat the oil to 190 degrees Celsius (375 degrees Fahrenheit).

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: Just before frying, prepare the batter. In a bowl, whisk together the fine corn flour, baking powder, and salt. Pour in the ice cold seltzer water and whisk lightly until just combined. Lumps are acceptable.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Working in batches of 4 or 5, dip the frozen guacamole scoops into the batter, ensuring they are fully coated. Immediately drop them into the hot oil. Fry for 90 seconds to 2 minutes until the exterior is golden and crispy, but the center remains cool.

steps.title: finish
steps.instruction: Use a spider skimmer to carefully remove the fried bites from the oil. Transfer them to a wire cooling rack set over paper towels to drain excess oil.

steps.title: finish
steps.instruction: Serve immediately while the outer shell is hot and the inside is still creamy and cold.

mexico.name: Classic Tres Leches Cake
mexico.description: A remarkably tender sponge cake soaked in a rich, sweet blend of three milks, crowned with billowy whipped cream and a dusting of warm cinnamon.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: Preheat the oven to 175°C/350°F. Lightly grease a 23x33 centimeter baking dish with non-stick spray or butter.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: In a large mixing bowl, using an electric mixer, whip the egg yolks with 150 grams of the granulated sugar until the mixture is pale yellow and has doubled in volume. Stir in the whole milk and vanilla extract.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: Sift the all-purpose flour, baking powder, and fine sea salt directly into the egg yolk mixture. Gently mix with a rubber spatula until just combined, taking care not to overmix.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: In a separate, impeccably clean medium mixing bowl, whip the egg whites with an electric mixer until soft peaks form. Gradually add the remaining 50 grams of granulated sugar and continue whipping until stiff, glossy peaks form.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: Add one-third of the whipped egg whites to the egg yolk batter to lighten it, folding gently. Fold in the remaining egg whites in two additions, using a sweeping motion from the bottom of the bowl over the top to retain as much air as possible.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Pour the airy batter into the prepared baking dish and gently smooth the top. Bake for 25 to 30 minutes. The cake is done when the center springs back to a gentle touch and reaches an internal temperature of 95°C/203°F.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Remove the cake from the oven and allow it to cool completely in the pan at room temperature. Do not rush this process as pouring cold milk over hot cake causes a rubbery texture.

steps.title: assemble
steps.instruction: Once the cake is completely cool, use a skewer or a fork to pierce holes generously all over the surface. Ensure the holes penetrate all the way to the bottom of the pan to allow even soaking.

steps.title: assemble
steps.instruction: In a medium mixing bowl, whisk together the evaporated milk, sweetened condensed milk, and 60 milliliters of heavy cream until the mixture is entirely homogeneous.

steps.title: assemble
steps.instruction: Slowly pour the three-milk mixture evenly over the pierced cake. Pay special attention to the edges, pausing as needed to allow the sponge to drink up the liquid before adding more.

steps.title: assemble
steps.instruction: Cover the baking dish tightly with plastic wrap or a lid and refrigerate for at least 4 hours, or ideally overnight, to allow the milks to soak perfectly into the crumb.

steps.title: finish
steps.instruction: When ready to serve, whip the 350 milliliters of heavy whipping cream, powdered sugar, and vanilla extract in a chilled bowl until stiff peaks form.

steps.title: finish
steps.instruction: Spread the whipped cream evenly over the surface of the soaked cake. Lightly dust the top with ground cinnamon. Keep refrigerated until the exact moment of serving.

mexico.name: Crispy Clam, Bacon, and Cheese Quesadillas
mexico.description: Smoky bacon and briny clams melt together with gooey Oaxaca cheese inside a perfectly blistered, golden-brown tortilla. Each bite delivers a deeply savory, crispy, and comforting coastal flavor profile perfect for a quick appetizer.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: Drain the canned chopped clams through a fine mesh strainer. Press down gently to extract excess liquid, then pat the clams aggressively with paper towels until almost completely dry.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Place the diced bacon in a cold cast iron skillet, then turn the heat to medium. Cook until the bacon is crispy and the fat has rendered, about 5 to 7 minutes.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: Using a slotted spoon, remove the bacon to a paper towel-lined plate. Carefully wipe the excess bacon grease from the skillet, leaving just a thin film for flavor.

steps.title: assemble
steps.instruction: In a medium bowl, combine the shredded cheese, dried clams, crispy bacon, sliced scallions, and minced jalapeno. Toss well to distribute the ingredients evenly throughout the cheese.

steps.title: assemble
steps.instruction: Lay the flour tortillas flat on a clean surface. Distribute the clam and cheese mixture evenly over one half of each tortilla, then fold the empty half over the filling to create half-moon shapes.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Heat the skillet over medium heat, approximately 175 degrees C or 350 degrees F. Lightly brush the skillet with melted butter. Place two folded tortillas in the pan and sear until the bottom is golden brown and blistered.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Carefully flip the quesadillas with a spatula. Continue to sear until the second side is crisp and the cheese interior is completely melted and gooey.

steps.title: finish
steps.instruction: Transfer the cooked quesadillas to a cutting board and let them rest for 1 to 2 minutes so the cheese sets slightly. Cut each half-moon into three wedges and serve immediately.

mexico.name: Spiced Fish Finger Tacos with Pickled Onions
mexico.description: Crispy fish fingers dusted with warm spices, nestled in soft tortillas, and topped with zesty lime crema and bright pickled red onions for a vibrant, satisfying bite.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: Preheat the oven to 200°C/400°F. Line a baking sheet with parchment paper to prevent sticking.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: Place the frozen fish fingers on the prepared baking sheet. Sprinkle evenly with smoked paprika, ground cumin, and garlic powder, tossing gently so the spices adhere to the frosty coating.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Bake in the preheated oven for 15 minutes, turning halfway through, until the exterior is golden and crispy, and the internal temperature reaches 74°C/165°F.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: While the fish fingers bake, combine the sour cream and fresh lime juice in a small bowl. Stir thoroughly to create a smooth, tangy crema.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Heat a dry frying pan over medium-high heat. Warm each flour tortilla for about 30 seconds per side until soft, pliable, and slightly charred at the edges.

steps.title: assemble
steps.instruction: Spread a generous spoonful of lime crema onto the center of each warm tortilla. Top with two spiced fish fingers, a heap of pickled red onions, and a sprinkle of fresh coriander. Serve immediately.

mexico.name: Cumin-Lime Shredded Pork Nachos
mexico.description: Crispy tortilla chips buckle under a mountain of tender, citrus-spiked pulled pork and molten cheddar cheese. Vibrant radishes, fresh cilantro, and a cooling lime crema cut through the rich, warming winter spices to deliver perfectly balanced bites.

ingredients.name: scallions
ingredients.amount: 40 g (thinly sliced, green and white parts)

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: Preheat the oven to 160C/320F for braising the pork. In a small bowl, combine the cumin, coriander, kosher salt, and black pepper. Toss the pork shoulder chunks in the spice blend until thoroughly coated.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Add the spiced pork chunks in batches to avoid crowding, searing until deeply browned on all sides.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Return all pork to the Dutch oven. Pour in the chicken broth and the first measure of lime juice, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom. Cover with a heavy lid, transfer to the 160C/320F oven, and braise until the pork yields entirely to a fork.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: Remove the pot from the oven and carefully transfer the pork to a cutting board. Shred the meat using two forks. Skim excess fat from the braising liquid, then return the shredded pork to the juices to keep it moist. Increase the oven temperature to 200C/400F.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: While the oven preheats, whisk together the sour cream, the second measure of lime juice, lime zest, and salt in a small mixing bowl. Set aside in the refrigerator to allow flavors to meld.

steps.title: assemble
steps.instruction: Arrange half of the tortilla chips in a slightly overlapping layer on a large baking sheet. Top evenly with half of the drained shredded pork and half of the grated cheddar cheese. Repeat with a second layer of chips, the remaining pork, and the remaining cheese.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Bake the assembled nachos in the 200C/400F oven until the cheese is completely melted, bubbling, and just starting to brown at the edges.

steps.title: finish
steps.instruction: Remove the nachos from the oven. Immediately scatter the sliced radishes, scallions, and chopped cilantro over the hot cheese. Generously drizzle or dollop the chilled lime crema across the entire platter. Serve immediately.

india.name: Classic Tamarind Chutney
india.description: A glossy, mahogany-colored condiment delivering a complex punch of sweet, tart, and deeply savory spiced notes. The quintessential dipping sauce for samosas, pakoras, and chaat.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: Combine the tamarind chunks and hot water in a medium saucepan. Bring to a rapid boil at 100 degrees Celsius (212 degrees Fahrenheit) over medium-high heat, then turn off the heat. Let the tamarind sit and soften for 5 minutes.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: Using a silicone spatula, mash the softened tamarind in the water. Pour the mixture through a fine-mesh strainer set over a mixing bowl. Press firmly on the solids to extract all the pulp. Discard the leftover fibers and incidental seeds.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Rinse the saucepan and return the strained tamarind liquid to it over medium heat. Add the crushed jaggery, roasted cumin powder, Kashmiri red chili powder, black salt, ground ginger, and kosher salt. Whisk vigorously to dissolve the jaggery and incorporate the spices.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Bring the mixture to a gentle bubble, then reduce the heat to low. Simmer uncovered, stirring occasionally, until the liquid reduces and thickens slightly. The chutney is ready when it lightly coats the back of a spoon.

steps.title: finish
steps.instruction: Remove the saucepan from the heat and allow the chutney to sit at room temperature. It will thicken considerably as it cools. Transfer to a clean glass jar once fully cooled.

india.name: Gulab Jamun Cheesecake
india.description: A decadent fusion dessert marrying the rich, spiced syrup of traditional Indian gulab jamun with the creamy elegance of a Western baked cheesecake, all resting on a cardamom-infused biscuit crust.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: Preheat the oven to 160°C or 320°F. Line the bottom and sides of a 20cm springform pan with parchment paper.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: Place the digestive biscuits in a food processor and pulse until fine crumbs form.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: Transfer the biscuit crumbs to a mixing bowl. Add the melted butter and green cardamom powder, stirring until the mixture is evenly moistened.

steps.title: assemble
steps.instruction: Press the crumb mixture firmly and evenly into the bottom of the prepared springform pan using the back of a spoon or a flat-bottomed glass.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Bake the crust at 160°C or 320°F for 10 minutes. Remove from the oven and let it cool completely.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: In the bowl of a stand mixer, beat the room temperature cream cheese on medium speed until perfectly smooth and free of lumps.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: Add the sweetened condensed milk, heavy cream, cornstarch, rose water, and saffron-infused milk to the cream cheese. Beat on low speed just until combined, scraping down the sides of the bowl as needed.

steps.title: assemble
steps.instruction: Arrange the drained, halved gulab jamun pieces evenly over the cooled biscuit crust, flat side down.

steps.title: assemble
steps.instruction: Gently pour the cheesecake batter over the gulab jamun pieces, spreading it into an even layer with a spatula.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Bake at 160°C or 320°F for 45 to 55 minutes. The cheesecake is done when the edges are set but the center 5cm still has a slight wobble when gently shaken.

steps.title: finish
steps.instruction: Turn off the oven and prop the door slightly open. Leave the cheesecake in the oven to cool slowly for 1 hour.

steps.title: finish
steps.instruction: Remove the cheesecake from the oven and transfer it to the refrigerator. Chill for at least 6 hours, or preferably overnight, until completely firm.

steps.title: finish
steps.instruction: Once fully chilled, carefully release the sides of the springform pan. Garnish the edges with chopped pistachios and dried edible rose petals before slicing.

india.name: Mango Buttermilk Sherbet
india.description: A refreshing, creamy, and tangy frozen dessert inspired by traditional mango lassi. Ripe mangoes and cultured buttermilk combine for a light tropical treat with a warm hint of cardamom.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: Place the mango puree, buttermilk, white sugar, lime juice, and ground cardamom into a blender. Blend on high speed for 1 to 2 minutes until completely smooth and the sugar is fully dissolved.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: Transfer the blended mixture to a sealed container and refrigerate for at least 1 hour. A thoroughly chilled base churns faster and produces a smoother final texture.

steps.title: finish
steps.instruction: Pour the chilled mixture into an ice cream maker. Churn according to the manufacturer instructions, typically 20 to 25 minutes, until it reaches the consistency of soft serve.

steps.title: finish
steps.instruction: Transfer the churned sherbet to a freezer-safe container. Smooth the top with a spatula and freeze at -18°C/0°F for at least 3 hours until firm enough to scoop.

india.name: Chai Masala Spiced Apple Cake
india.description: A comforting, aromatic cake combining the tender crumb of a classic apple bake with the deep, warming spices of traditional Indian masala chai. Notes of ginger, green cardamom, cinnamon, and a hint of black pepper elevate sweet, tart apples.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: Preheat the oven to 175 degrees Celsius or 350 degrees Fahrenheit. Grease a 20cm round cake pan and line the bottom with parchment paper.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: In a medium mixing bowl, whisk together the flour, baking powder, baking soda, salt, ground ginger, cardamom, cinnamon, cloves, and black pepper. Remove 1 tablespoon of this mixture and set it aside to coat the apples.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: Toss the diced apples with the reserved tablespoon of the dry flour mixture. This prevents the fruit from sinking to the bottom of the cake during baking.

steps.title: mix
steps.instruction: In a large mixing bowl, beat the softened butter, granulated sugar, and brown sugar with an electric hand mixer on medium speed until light and fluffy.

steps.title: mix
steps.instruction: Add the eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition and scraping down the sides of the bowl. Stir in the vanilla extract.

steps.title: mix
steps.instruction: Turn the mixer to low speed. Add one-third of the dry flour mixture, followed by half of the yogurt. Add another third of the flour, the remaining yogurt, and finish with the last of the flour. Mix only until just combined to avoid a tough cake.

steps.title: assemble
steps.instruction: Gently fold the flour-coated diced apples into the batter using a rubber spatula.

steps.title: assemble
steps.instruction: Transfer the batter into the prepared cake pan, smoothing the top into an even layer with the spatula.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Bake in the preheated oven at 175 degrees Celsius or 350 degrees Fahrenheit for 45 to 50 minutes, or until a wooden skewer inserted into the center comes out clean.

steps.title: finish
steps.instruction: Allow the cake to cool in the pan for 10 minutes. Run a knife around the edges, then carefully invert onto a wire cooling rack to cool completely before serving.

india.name: Hearty Vegetable Curry with Homemade Naan
india.description: A vibrant, aromatic vegetable curry simmered in a rich tomato and coconut sauce, paired with warm, pillowy homemade naan bread blistered to perfection on a cast iron skillet.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: In a mixing bowl, combine the warm water (40C/105F), active dry yeast, and granulated sugar. Stir gently and let sit until foamy, indicating the yeast is active.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: Add the bread flour, plain yogurt, and salt to the yeast mixture. Knead until a smooth, slightly tacky dough forms.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: Cover the mixing bowl with a damp cloth and place in a warm spot to proof until the dough has doubled in size.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: While the dough proofs, heat coconut oil in a Dutch oven over medium heat. Add the diced onion, minced garlic, and minced ginger. Sauté until the onions are soft and translucent.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Add the garam masala, coriander, cumin, turmeric, and salt to the Dutch oven. Stir constantly to toast the spices and prevent burning.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Pour in the diced tomatoes with juices, coconut milk, and vegetable broth. Scrape the bottom of the pot to lift any stuck spices. Add the potatoes, cauliflower, and carrots. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to maintain a simmer.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Stir the frozen green peas into the curry. Simmer just until the peas are heated through, then turn off the heat and cover to keep warm.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: Punch down the proofed dough and divide into four equal portions. Use a rolling pin to flatten each portion into an oval shape about half a centimeter thick.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Preheat a cast iron skillet over high heat until smoking (approximately 260C/500F). Place one piece of rolled dough in the dry skillet. Cook until large bubbles form on top, then flip and cook the other side.

steps.title: assemble
steps.instruction: Brush the warm naan generously with melted ghee. Ladle the hot vegetable curry into bowls, garnish with freshly chopped cilantro, and serve immediately with the bread.

india.name: Grilled Paneer Tikka Skewers
india.description: Smoky, charred skewers of tender paneer cheese, crisp bell peppers, and sweet onions, all marinated in a vibrantly spiced, tangy yogurt base.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: Cut the paneer, red bell pepper, green bell pepper, and red onion into uniform 3cm pieces to ensure even cooking and easy skewering.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: In a large mixing bowl, whisk together the greek yogurt, mustard oil, ginger garlic paste, kashmiri chili powder, garam masala, ground coriander, turmeric, chaat masala, kasuri methi, lemon juice, and salt until smooth and vibrant red.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: Add the chopped paneer and vegetables to the mixing bowl. Gently toss the ingredients until every piece is thickly coated in the yogurt marinade.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: Cover the bowl tightly and refrigerate to allow the spices to penetrate the paneer and vegetables. Marinate for at least 2 hours, or up to 24 hours for maximum flavor.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Heat a grill or grill pan over medium-high heat until it reaches roughly 200°C/400°F. Lightly brush the grates with a high smoke point oil.

steps.title: assemble
steps.instruction: Thread the marinated paneer, red onion, and bell peppers alternately onto the skewers, packing them snugly but not too tight to ensure heat circulates evenly.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Place the skewers on the hot grill. Cook, turning every 2 to 3 minutes, until the paneer develops a crisp, beautifully charred exterior and the vegetables are tender but still retain a slight bite.

steps.title: finish
steps.instruction: Transfer the hot skewers to a serving platter. Serve immediately while the paneer is soft and warm inside.

thailand.name: Classic Thai Mango Sticky Rice
thailand.description: A beloved Thai dessert featuring perfectly steamed glutinous rice drenched in a rich, sweet, and slightly salty coconut cream sauce, paired with luscious slices of ripe mango and topped with crispy toasted mung beans.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: Rinse the glutinous rice under cold water until the water runs completely clear. Submerge the rice in ample water and soak at room temperature for at least 4 hours, or overnight.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Drain the soaked rice. Line a steamer with damp cheesecloth, place the rice inside, and steam over rolling boiling water at 100°C/212°F for 25 to 30 minutes until the rice is tender and translucent.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: While the rice is steaming, combine 250ml coconut milk, palm sugar, 3g salt, and the pandan leaf in a medium saucepan. Simmer gently over medium heat until the sugar dissolves completely.

steps.title: assemble
steps.instruction: Transfer the hot steamed rice to a mixing bowl. Immediately pour the hot sweet coconut sauce over the rice and gently fold to combine. Cover the bowl and let it rest for 20 minutes to absorb the liquid.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: In a small bowl, mix the rice flour and water to create a smooth slurry.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: In a small saucepan, heat the remaining 120ml coconut milk and 2g salt. Whisk in the rice flour slurry and simmer gently until the mixture thickens slightly.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: Peel the mangoes and slice the flesh diagonally into bite-sized pieces, taking care to cut around the flat inner pit.

steps.title: finish
steps.instruction: Place a portion of the warm sticky rice on a plate next to the sliced mango. Drizzle the salty coconut topping over the rice and sprinkle with toasted mung beans.

thailand.name: Chicken Pad See Ew
thailand.description: A classic Thai stir-fried noodle dish featuring chewy wide rice noodles, tender marinated chicken, and crisp Chinese broccoli, all tossed in a savory, slightly sweet, and deeply caramelized soy sauce.

ingredients.name: chinese broccoli
ingredients.amount: 150 g (leaves roughly chopped, stems sliced thinly)

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: In a mixing bowl, combine the sliced chicken breast, light soy sauce, and cornstarch. Mix thoroughly until the chicken is evenly coated. Set aside to marinate for 15 minutes.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: In a small bowl, whisk together the dark soy sauce, oyster sauce, light soy sauce, granulated sugar, white vinegar, and water until the sugar is fully dissolved.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Heat half of the neutral oil in a carbon steel wok over high heat until it begins to smoke. Add the marinated chicken and spread it into a single layer. Sear without moving for 1 minute to develop color, then stir continuously until the chicken is cooked through and reaches an internal temperature of 74C or 165F. Remove the chicken from the wok and set aside.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Wipe the wok clean if necessary, then add the remaining oil over medium-high heat. Add the minced garlic and sliced Chinese broccoli stems. Saute for 30 seconds until fragrant and slightly softened.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Push the aromatics and stems to one side of the wok. Pour the lightly beaten eggs into the empty space. Allow them to set for a few seconds, then break them apart and scramble until fully cooked.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Increase the heat to high. Add the separated fresh rice noodles, Chinese broccoli leaves, cooked chicken, and the prepared stir-fry sauce. Toss rapidly and continuously to ensure the noodles are evenly coated in the sauce and the leaves wilt.

steps.title: finish
steps.instruction: Stop stirring and spread the noodles into an even layer in the wok. Let them sit completely undisturbed for 15 to 20 seconds to caramelize the soy sauce and develop char on the edges of the noodles. Give one final toss to distribute the charred bits.

steps.title: finish
steps.instruction: Remove the wok from the heat immediately. Portion the noodles into bowls and serve hot.

thailand.name: Khao Tang Na Tang (Thai Rice Crackers with Creamy Shrimp & Chicken Dip)
thailand.description: Crispy, airy rice crackers served with a rich, savory-sweet coconut dip featuring minced chicken, shrimp, and roasted peanuts. This classic Thai appetizer balances the crunch of the rice with a luscious, aromatic sauce infused with coriander root, garlic, and white pepper.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: Make the 'Sam Kluer' paste: Pound white peppercorns in a mortar until fine. Add coriander roots and garlic cloves, pounding until a smooth, uniform paste forms.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Heat 15ml vegetable oil in a wok or saucepan over medium heat. Add the pounded paste and sliced shallots. Sauté until the shallots soften and the mixture is highly aromatic.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Add the minced chicken and shrimp to the pan. Stir vigorously to break up any clumps and cook until the meat turns opaque.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Pour in the coconut milk, then season with palm sugar, fish sauce, and tamarind paste. Bring to a gentle simmer. Add the coarsely ground peanuts and stir to combine.

steps.title: finish
steps.instruction: Reduce heat to low and let the dip simmer gently until it reaches a coating consistency. Taste and adjust seasoning; it should be predominantly sweet and salty with a hint of nutty richness. Remove from heat.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Clean the wok or use a separate pot. Heat frying oil to 190°C/375°F. Fry the dried rice cakes in batches. They will expand instantly. Flip once and remove immediately (within 5-10 seconds) to drain on a wire rack.

steps.title: assemble
steps.instruction: Transfer the warm dip to a serving bowl. Garnish with sliced red spur chilies and coriander leaves. Serve alongside the crispy rice crackers for dipping.

thailand.name: Warming Vegetarian Thai Tom Yum Soup with Tofu and Mushrooms
thailand.description: A vibrant, aromatic broth infused with lemongrass, galangal, and makrut lime leaves. Silken tofu and earthy mushrooms float in this spicy, sour, and deeply comforting plant-based take on the classic Thai soup.

steps.title: prep
steps.instruction: Using the back of a chef's knife or a heavy object, firmly bruise the lemongrass stalks and smash the Thai chilies and garlic cloves. This ruptures the cell walls and allows the essential oils to release into the soup.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Place a heavy-bottomed pot over medium-high heat. Add the vegetable stock and bring it to a rolling boil at approximately 100 degrees C or 212 degrees F.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Add the bruised lemongrass, sliced galangal, torn makrut lime leaves, smashed garlic, and chilies to the boiling stock. Lower the heat to maintain a gentle bubbling and simmer to infuse the broth with the aromatics.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Stir in the oyster mushrooms, halved cherry tomatoes, and the vegetarian roasted chili paste if you are using it. Continue to cook until the mushrooms begin to soften and the tomatoes gently collapse.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Add the tamari and chopped palm sugar to the pot. Stir gently until the sugar dissolves completely into the hot liquid.

steps.title: cook
steps.instruction: Carefully slide the tofu cubes into the pot. Let the soup gently bubble for a few minutes just to heat the tofu all the way through without causing it to break apart.

steps.title: finish
steps.instruction: Remove the pot entirely from the heat. Stir in the fresh lime juice. Taste the broth carefully and adjust the seasoning with an extra splash of tamari for saltiness or lime juice for acidity until it hits a perfect balance.

steps.title: finish
steps.instruction: Use a ladle to divide the soup into warmed serving bowls, taking care to distribute the tofu and mushrooms evenly. Leave the tough herbs in the pot or instruct guests not to eat them. Garnish heavily with fresh chopped cilantro.
